name: Coffee-Walnut Toffee
recipeIngredient: 4 1/2 oz imported bittersweet chocolate, finely chopped
4 1/2 oz imported white chocolate (such as Lindt), finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Butter a small cookie sheet.
2. Melt butter in a heavy 2-1/2-quart saucepan over low heat.
3. Add both sugars, water, molasses, espresso powder, cinnamon, and salt; stir until sugar dissolves.
4. Attach a clip-on candy thermometer to the pan.
5. Increase heat to medium. Cook until the thermometer registers 290°F, stirring slowly but constantly and scraping the bottom of the pan with a wooden spatula for about 20 minutes.
6. Remove the pan from heat. Mix in the toasted walnuts.
7. Immediately pour the mixture onto the prepared cookie sheet. Do not scrape the pan.
8. Tilt the sheet so that the toffee spreads to a thickness of about 1/4 inch.
9. Sprinkle chocolates by generous tablespoons atop the toffee, alternating bittersweet and white chocolates.
10. Let stand for 1 minute. Using the back of a spoon, swirl the chocolates to spread slightly.
11. Shake the sheet to form an even chocolate layer. Use the tip of a knife to swirl the chocolates to create a marble pattern.
12. Sprinkle with the remaining chopped walnuts.
13. Refrigerate until the toffee is firm, about 1 hour.
14. Break into pieces.
(Alternatively, this can be made up to 2 weeks in advance and chilled in an airtight container. Serve cold or at room temperature. Arrange the pieces in large glass canning jars and wrap ribbons around each one for a festive presentation.)
